info.name: Spring IAM
info.description: Postman collection for the Spring Boot IAM service (authentication + authorization/PDP). Log in once via Auth > Login; the test script stores the access token in the accessToken collection variable and every other request reuses it. PDP/internal endpoints use X-Internal-Api-Key = {{internalApiKey}}; the service manifest sync uses {{serviceApiKey}}. The refresh token is a __Host-Refresh cookie handled automatically by Postman.

request.description: Create a new user account. Email verification required before login.

Authenticate and receive an RS256 access token. The test script stores data.accessToken into the accessToken collection variable. The refresh token is set as a __Host-Refresh cookie (handled by Postman).

exec: // Save the access token from a successful login so every other
// request in this collection is authenticated automatically.
console.warn('No data.accessToken found in login response');
console.error('Failed to parse login response: ' + e);

request.description: Rotate the access token using the __Host-Refresh cookie. No body — Postman sends the cookie automatically.
Swap the single-use code from the OAuth redirect for tokens.
Invalidate the current access token and clear the refresh cookie.
Revoke all refresh tokens (sessions) for the current user.
Requires current password; a verification link is sent to the new address.
Verify an invited user's email and set the password in one step.
List available OAuth providers (only when iam.features.oauth2=true).
Browser redirect to Google OAuth. Open in a browser, not as an API call.
Public signing keys for local JWT verification by consuming services.
SuperAdmin only. New key signs immediately; the old key verifies during its grace window.
Deployment feature discovery so clients can hide disabled modules.
The hot-path PDP decision endpoint. Internal API key or system JWT.
Evaluate up to 50 authorization requests in one call.
All effective permissions for a subject at a scope. Optional ?asOf for point-in-time reconstruction.
Reduce a list of resource ids to the subset the subject may act on (max 500).
Admin-only dry-run trace of the decision pipeline; writes no audit record.
What-if evaluation with a hypothetical assignment set. Admin only.
Who holds permission P at scope S. Admin only.
Recent authorization decisions for a subject. Senior admins / audit viewers.
SuperAdmin only. Optional ?domain filter.
Accepts a single object or an array. Key must match domain.resource.action.
Replaces the role's permission set with the supplied permission ids.
SuperAdmin only. Optional ?type filter.
Make child role inherit the parent role's permissions.
Assign a role to a subject within a scope.
Explicit deny that overrides any grant.
SuperAdmin only.
Per-instance grant. Feature-flagged (iam.features.resource-grants).
SuperAdmin only. Feature-flagged (iam.features.groups).
SuperAdmin only. Returns the API key ONCE. Feature-flagged (iam.features.service-registry).
SuperAdmin only.
Idempotent upsert of the service's own permission keys. Authenticated by the service's own API key (X-Internal-Api-Key: {{serviceApiKey}}) or a SuperAdmin bearer token.
Emergency time-boxed elevated access. Feature-flagged (iam.features.break-glass).
RFC 7662-shaped signature + expiry + revocation check. Internal callers only. Feature-flagged (iam.features.introspection).
Sessions revoked since a timestamp. Internal callers only. Feature-flagged (iam.features.revocation-feed).
Active device sessions for the current user.
Log out one device by session id.
Scopes where the current user has active role assignments (org switcher).
Effective permissions for the current user at the given scope.
